title: Why We Should Ban Ads Targeting Children
subtitle: 应禁止针对儿童的广告

paragraphs.en: How on earth did we come to this? We protect our children obsessively from every harm; we scrutinize every carer, teacher or doctor with whom they come into contact. Yet despite all this, one group, which in no way has their best interests at heart, has almost unconstrained access. We seem to take it for granted that advertisers are allowed to condition even the youngest children. Before they've developed a proper sense of identity or learned to handle money, they're encouraged to associate status and self-worth with stuff.
paragraphs.zh: 我们究竟何以至此？我们执迷地保护孩子免受一切伤害，仔细审查他们接触的每一位看护、老师或医生。然而尽管如此，有一个根本不把孩子利益放在心上的群体却几乎拥有不受限制的接触权。我们似乎理所当然地认为广告商可以调教甚至是最年幼的儿童。在他们形成正确身份认同或学会管理金钱之前，就被鼓励将地位和自我价值与物质挂钩。

paragraphs.en: We're turning out little consumers rather than young citizens who will value themselves for what they contribute to society. We've reached this point so gradually that many of us have never questioned it. It's crept up on us in the 60 years since advertisers started to target the young and found they could recruit them to a commercial assault on their parents. We've come to know it as 'pester power' — the ability of children to pressure parents to make certain purchases.
paragraphs.zh: 我们培养出的是小消费者，而非因对社会有所贡献而珍视自身的年轻公民。我们一步步走到这一步，以至于许多人从未质疑过。六十年来，自从广告商开始瞄准年轻人并发现可以招募他们向父母发动商业攻势，这一现象便悄然降临。我们已将其称为“纠缠力”——儿童迫使父母购买特定商品的能力。

paragraphs.en: Many psychologists and child development experts point to research suggesting that this cradle-to-grave consumerism is contributing to growing rates of low self-esteem, depression and other mental illnesses. Not all psychologists agree. There're plenty working hand in glove with a £12 billion-a-year industry that has turned the manipulation of emotions and desires into an art form. It's also one that's forever developing new ways to persuade our children to desire material possessions.
paragraphs.zh: 许多心理学家和儿童发展专家指出，研究表明这种从摇篮到坟墓的消费主义正导致自尊低下、抑郁和其他精神疾病的发病率不断上升。并非所有心理学家都同意。有大量人与这个年产值120亿英镑的行业密切合作，该行业已将操纵情感和欲望变成了一门艺术。它还不断开发新方法说服我们的孩子渴望物质财富。

paragraphs.en: Advertising can serve a useful purpose for children. Marketing may help socialize children as consumers, inform them about products, and help them carve out unique identities as they reach adulthood. Then, should we ban all advertising aimed at young children? I say yes. Of course there'll be plenty of objections to an outright ban on advertising to the under-11s. There'll be those who argue it would breach freedom of speech and infringe corporate rights.
paragraphs.zh: 广告对儿童也有有益的一面。营销可以帮助儿童作为消费者社会化，让他们了解产品，并在成年时帮助塑造独特身份。那么，我们是否应禁止所有针对幼儿的广告？我认为是的。当然，彻底禁止向11岁以下儿童投放广告会招致大量反对。会有人认为这违反言论自由并侵犯企业权利。

paragraphs.en: Most parents hate what advertising does to their children, but we do have the power to end it and let our children grow up free from many of the pressures of consumerism until they're old enough to make their own decisions. And though advertising is only part of an all-pervasive marketing culture, we need to make a start somewhere. Let's ban all advertising targeting children of primary school age and younger now.
paragraphs.zh: 大多数父母痛恨广告对孩子的所作所为，但我们确实有能力终结它，让我们的孩子在成长中摆脱消费主义的诸多压力，直到他们足够大能自己做决定。尽管广告只是无处不在的营销文化的一部分，我们总得从某处开始。我们现在就禁止所有针对小学及以下年龄儿童的广告吧。
